text: Anomaly Detection in Battery Degradation Trajectories WHAT CONSTITUTES AN ANOMALY In battery degradation, an anomaly is a cycle where the measured capacity or derived features deviate significantly from the expected degradation trajectory. Anomalies may indicate: unexpected rapid fade (accelerated aging event), measurement artifact (sensor fault, test equipment issue), unusual operating conditions (temperature excursion, over-discharge), or internal cell events (lithium plating episode, micro-short circuit). Anomalies must be distinguished from natural variability. Discharge capacity varies cycle-to-cycle due to temperature fluctuations, SOC variation at cycle start, and measurement noise (typically ±0.5-1% of rated capacity). Statistical anomaly detection methods set thresholds that balance sensitivity (detecting real anomalies) against specificity (avoiding false alarms on normal varia

ates observed RUL. An anomaly score above 50 cycles indicates a strong deviation; scores above 30 cycles are worth monitoring; below 10 cycles falls within normal prediction uncertainty. TRAJECTORY SHAPE ANOMALIES Beyond point anomalies (single cycle deviations), trajectory shape anomalies capture sustained changes in the degradation rate. A sudden inflection in the capacity-cycle curve (knee-point acceleration) is a critical trajectory anomaly: it signals that the battery has entered a rapid degradation phase. The knee point in LiCoO2/graphite batteries typically occurs when lithium inventory is exhausted beyond the cathode-limited region, triggering accelerated SEI growth and mechanical damage. Detecting the knee point 20-50 cycles before end-of-life is the highest-value anomaly detection task. BATTERY B0036 ANOMALY INTERPRETATION Battery B0036 shows 32 anomalous cycles, concentrated i

# System Summary — Battery AI Co-Scientist This system analyzes lithium-ion battery degradation data to predict Remaining Useful Life (RUL) in a safe, auditable, and uncertainty-aware manner. ## What the system does 1. Standardizes raw metadata and time-series battery data 2. Computes cycle-level degradation features 3. Estimates Remaining Useful Life (RUL) 4. Trains statistical and machine-learning models 5. Quantifies uncertainty and assigns risk levels 6. Generates model-traceable explanations and counterfactuals 7. Produces a supervisor-audited final verdict ## Key guarantees - No predictions are made without uncertainty intervals - No explanations are generated without model evidence - All schema mappings are validated before modeling - All explanations are labeled as hypotheses, not causal claims ## Outputs - RUL predictions with confidence bounds - Risk categories (LOW / MEDIUM /
